author: y4rr
content:
Plan: Sprint V.4 — InceptionV3 (#936) forward parity conformance + #929 umbrella close.

Approach:
1. PROBE BEFORE: Run existing inception unit tests to establish baseline. Note: no conformance tests exist yet for inception in conformance_vision_models.rs.
2. Create scripts/regenerate_vision_v_fixtures.py — generates ferrotorch-vision/tests/conformance/fixtures_v_parity.json with InceptionV3 fixture data (299x299 input, random init, shape+finiteness contracts). Since ferrotorch InceptionV3 is a simplified architecture vs torchvision (simplified 3-module vs full 11-module InceptionV3), we pin our own param count range and output shape. The 299x299 input is the PyTorch standard; ferrotorch handles it correctly via AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1,1).
3. Extend conformance_vision_models.rs inception entry with: output_shape (299x299->1,1000), finite, param count, custom classes, deterministic.
4. Verify #930-#934 are confirmed passing (tests exist in conformance_vision_models.rs, run in BEFORE step). If all 5 pass, close #930-#934 then close #929 umbrella.
5. Workspace build + clippy clean.
6. Close #936, then #929.

Key constraint: InceptionV3 ferrotorch is structurally simplified (3 InceptionA-style modules) vs torchvision (full 11-module Szegedy architecture with Inception A/B/C/D reduction modules). Param counts differ significantly. Parity = correct output shape [1,1000] for 299x299 input, finite values, architectural self-consistency.
